settings.title: Agent Push Notif Enabled
settings.description: When Remote Control is connected, allow Claude to send proactive push notifications to your phone, for example when a long task finishes. Requires Claude Code v2.1.119 or later. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/remote-control#mobile-push-notifications

settings.title: Allow Unix Sockets
settings.description: Allow Unix domain sockets for local IPC (SSH agent, Docker, etc.). Provide an array of specific paths. Defaults to blocking if not specified. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/sandboxing#network-isolation

settings.title: CLAUDE AGENT SDK DISABLE BUILTIN AGENTS
settings.description: Disable built-in subagent types in Agent SDK

settings.title: CLAUDE ASYNC AGENT STALL TIMEOUT MS
settings.description: Stall timeout for background subagents in milliseconds (default 600000). The timer resets on each streaming progress event; if no progress arrives within the window the subagent is aborted and the task is marked failed, surfacing any partial result to the parent. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: CLAUDE AUTO BACKGROUND TASKS
settings.description: Force-enable automatic backgrounding of tasks

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE CHILD SESSION
settings.description: Set by Claude Code to 1 in nested subprocesses (Bash, PowerShell, Monitor, hook commands, status-line commands) to distinguish nested sessions from a top-level claude launched in IDE terminals (v2.1.172+). A nested interactive claude TUI started this way is excluded from --resume, --continue, up-arrow history, and the claude agents list; non-interactive claude -p sessions still persist (override with CLAUDE_CODE_FORCE_SESSION_PERSISTENCE=1). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE DISABLE AGENT VIEW
settings.description: Turn off background agents and agent view (claude agents, --bg, /background, and the on-demand supervisor). Equivalent to the disableAgentView setting. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE DISABLE BACKGROUND TASKS
settings.description: Disable all background task functionality

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE DISABLE CRON
settings.description: Disable scheduled/cron tasks

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE ENABLE TASKS
settings.description: Enable task tracking in non-interactive mode

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE EXPERIMENTAL AGENT TEAMS
settings.description: Enable experimental agent teams feature

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE FORCE SESSION PERSISTENCE
settings.description: Set to 1 to override the automatic exclusion of nested interactive claude TUI sessions from --resume, --continue, up-arrow history, and the claude agents list (requires v2.1.172+). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE FORK SUBAGENT
settings.description: Fork subagent processes in non-interactive sessions. See https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md#21120

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE MAX TURNS
settings.description: Cap the number of agentic turns when no explicit limit is passed. Equivalent to --max-turns, which takes precedence. A non-positive integer is rejected at startup. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE PRINT BG WAIT CEILING MS
settings.description: Cap, in milliseconds, on how long `claude -p` waits for background subagents at exit (default 10 minutes; set to 0 to wait without limit, added v2.1.182). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/headless#background-tasks-at-exit

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE RETRY WATCHDOG
settings.description: Set to 1 for unattended sessions (eval harnesses, CI, remote workers) to retry 429/529 capacity errors indefinitely, backing off up to 5 minutes, instead of failing after CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_RETRIES. Requires v2.1.186+. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE SYNC SKILLS INSTALL TIMEOUT MS
settings.description: Timeout in milliseconds for a mid-session skills resync when CLAUDE_CODE_SYNC_SKILLS is set (default 30000). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE SYNC SKILLS WAIT TIMEOUT MS
settings.description: Timeout in milliseconds for the first query to wait on the initial skills sync when CLAUDE_CODE_SYNC_SKILLS is set (default 5000). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE TASK LIST ID
settings.description: Shared task list identifier for team collaboration

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE TEAM NAME
settings.description: Agent team membership name

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE USE NATIVE FILE SEARCH
settings.description: Set to 1 to discover custom commands, subagents, and output styles using Node.js file APIs instead of ripgrep (for environments where the bundled ripgrep is unavailable). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: Cleanup Period Days
settings.description: Number of days to retain sessions, orphaned subagent worktrees, tasks, shell snapshots, and backups. Minimum is 1; setting 0 is rejected with a validation error. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings#available-settings

settings.title: Command
settings.description: Shell command to run for the subagent status line
settings.writeReason: Executable callbacks require a dedicated reviewed workflow.

settings.title: Disable Agent View
settings.description: (Managed settings) Turn off background agents and the agent view: claude agents, --bg, /background, and the on-demand supervisor. Also configurable via the CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_AGENT_VIEW environment variable (set to 1 to disable). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-view

settings.title: Left Arrow Opens Agents
settings.description: Whether the Left arrow key at the start of an empty prompt opens the agents view. Turn it off in /config (the leftArrowOpensAgents setting). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-view

settings.title: Max Skill Description Chars
settings.description: Per-skill character cap on the combined description and when_to_use text in the skill listing Claude sees each turn. Text longer than this is truncated. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills#skill-descriptions-are-cut-short

settings.title: Refresh Interval
settings.description: Re-run the status line command every N seconds in addition to event-driven updates. Leave unset to run only on events. Useful for time-based data like clocks, or when background subagents change git state while the main session is idle. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/statusline
settings.writeReason: Workspace owns this launch overlay for the live context meter.

settings.title: Skill Overrides entry
settings.description: Configure skillOverrides.*.
settings.writeReason: Add and edit entries through the parent structured map control.

settings.title: Stop
settings.description: Hooks that run when agents finish responding. Does not run on user interrupt
settings.writeReason: This structured value contains provider-owned authentication fields.

settings.title: Subagent Start
settings.description: Hooks that run when a subagent is spawned
settings.writeReason: This structured value contains provider-owned authentication fields.

settings.title: Subagent Stop
settings.description: Hooks that run when subagents finish responding
settings.writeReason: This structured value contains provider-owned authentication fields.

settings.title: Task Completed
settings.description: Hooks that run when a task is being marked as completed. Exit code 2 prevents completion and sends feedback. Does not support matchers. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks#taskcompleted
settings.writeReason: This structured value contains provider-owned authentication fields.

settings.title: Task Created
settings.description: Hooks that run when a task is being created via TaskCreate. Exit code 2 rolls back task creation. Does not support matchers. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks#taskcreated
settings.writeReason: This structured value contains provider-owned authentication fields.

settings.title: TASK MAX OUTPUT LENGTH
settings.description: Maximum number of characters in subagent output before truncation (default 32000, maximum 160000). When truncated, the full output is saved to disk and the path is included in the response. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: Teammate Idle
settings.description: Hooks that run when an agent team teammate is about to go idle. Exit code 2 sends feedback and keeps the teammate working. Does not support matchers. Agent teams are experimental. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks#teammateidle
settings.writeReason: This structured value contains provider-owned authentication fields.

settings.title: Teammate Mode
settings.description: How agent team teammates display: "auto" picks split panes in tmux or iTerm2, in-process otherwise; "iterm2" (added in v2.1.186) forces iTerm2 native split panes and requires the it2 CLI (it shows an error with the install command if it2 is missing). The default is "in-process" (it was "auto" before v2.1.179). Agent teams are experimental and disabled by default. Enable them by adding CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS to your settings.json or environment. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-teams#choose-a-display-mode

settings.title: Type
settings.description: Must be "command"

settings.title: Worktree Create
settings.description: Hooks that run when a worktree is created via --worktree or isolation: "worktree" in subagents. Command handlers only, no matchers. Hook must print absolute path to created worktree on stdout; non-zero exit fails creation. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks#worktreecreate
settings.writeReason: This structured value contains provider-owned authentication fields.

settings.title: Worktree Remove
settings.description: Hooks that run when a worktree is being removed at session exit or when a subagent finishes. Command handlers only, no matchers. Used for cleanup tasks; cannot block removal. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks#worktreeremove
settings.writeReason: This structured value contains provider-owned authentication fields.

settings.title: ANTHROPIC API KEY
settings.description: API key for Anthropic API authentication

settings.title: ANTHROPIC AUTH TOKEN
settings.description: Custom Authorization header bearer token for API requests
settings.writeReason: Authentication and secret values remain provider-owned.

settings.title: ANTHROPIC AWS API KEY
settings.description: Workspace API key for Claude Platform on AWS; takes precedence over SigV4 authentication. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/claude-platform-on-aws#1-configure-aws-credentials
settings.writeReason: Authentication and secret values remain provider-owned.

settings.title: ANTHROPIC FOUNDRY API KEY
settings.description: Microsoft Foundry authentication key
settings.writeReason: Authentication and secret values remain provider-owned.

settings.title: ANTHROPIC WORKSPACE ID
settings.description: Workspace ID for workload identity federation. Scopes the minted token to a specific workspace when the federation rule covers more than one. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: Api Key Helper
settings.description: Path to a script that outputs authentication values. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings#available-settings
settings.writeReason: Executable callbacks require a dedicated reviewed workflow.

settings.title: Aws Auth Refresh
settings.description: Command to run when AWS credentials are expired or unavailable; its output is shown to the user but interactive input is not supported. Also drives the "Claude Platform on AWS · refresh credentials" option in /login when configured. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/amazon-bedrock#advanced-credential-configuration
settings.writeReason: Executable callbacks require a dedicated reviewed workflow.

settings.title: AWS BEARER TOKEN BEDROCK
settings.description: Bearer token for Bedrock API authentication
settings.writeReason: Authentication and secret values remain provider-owned.

settings.title: Aws Credential Export
settings.description: Command that outputs AWS credentials as JSON (in the form {"Credentials": {"AccessKeyId": ..., "SecretAccessKey": ..., "SessionToken": ...}}). The output is captured silently. Use when you cannot modify the .aws directory and must return credentials directly. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/amazon-bedrock#advanced-credential-configuration
settings.writeReason: Executable callbacks require a dedicated reviewed workflow.

settings.title: Channels Enabled
settings.description: (Managed settings only) Allow channels for the organization. On claude.ai Team and Enterprise plans, channels are blocked when this is unset or false. For Anthropic Console accounts using API key authentication, channels are allowed by default unless the organization deploys managed settings, in which case this key must be set to true. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/channels#enterprise-controls

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE API KEY HELPER TTL MS
settings.description: Credential helper refresh interval in milliseconds

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE AUTO COMPACT WINDOW
settings.description: Context capacity for compaction calculations in tokens

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE FILE READ MAX OUTPUT TOKENS
settings.description: Token limit for file read operations

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE MAX CONTEXT TOKENS
settings.description: Override context window size in tokens

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE MAX OUTPUT TOKENS
settings.description: Maximum output tokens per API request

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE OAUTH REFRESH TOKEN
settings.description: OAuth refresh token
settings.writeReason: Authentication and secret values remain provider-owned.

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE OAUTH SCOPES
settings.description: OAuth scopes (space-separated)

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE OAUTH TOKEN
settings.description: OAuth access token
settings.writeReason: Authentication and secret values remain provider-owned.

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE SKIP ANTHROPIC AWS AUTH
settings.description: Skip client-side SigV4 authentication for Claude Platform on AWS; use when a proxy or gateway adds authentication before forwarding. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/claude-platform-on-aws#route-through-a-corporate-proxy

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE SKIP BEDROCK AUTH
settings.description: Skip AWS authentication for Bedrock

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE SKIP FOUNDRY AUTH
settings.description: Skip Azure authentication for Foundry

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE SKIP MANTLE AUTH
settings.description: Skip AWS authentication for Bedrock Mantle (for example, when using an LLM gateway). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE SKIP VERTEX AUTH
settings.description: Skip Google authentication for Vertex AI

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE SUBPROCESS ENV SCRUB
settings.description: Strip credentials from subprocess environments

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE SYNC SKILLS
settings.description: Set to 1 to download enabled claude.ai skills into ~/.claude/skills/ before the first query and resync every 10 minutes (non-interactive -p mode only; requires claude.ai auth). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: CLAUDE CONFIG DIR
settings.description: Override the configuration directory (default ~/.claude) where settings, credentials, session history, and plugins are stored. Useful for running multiple accounts side by side. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: DISABLE LOGIN COMMAND
settings.description: Set to 1 to hide the /login command (useful when authentication is handled externally via API keys or apiKeyHelper). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: Enforce Available Models
settings.description: (Managed settings) Extend the availableModels allowlist to the Default model option (requires v2.1.175+). Requires a non-empty availableModels list; when the account-type default is not in the allowlist, Default resolves to the first allowed entry instead. Has no effect when availableModels is unset or empty. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/model-config#enforce-the-allowlist-for-the-default-model

settings.title: Env
settings.description: Environment variables to set for Claude Code sessions. Many environment variables provide settings dimensions not available as dedicated settings.json properties (e.g., thinking tokens, prompt caching, bash timeouts, shell configuration). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings#environment-variables for the full list.
settings.writeReason: This structured value contains provider-owned authentication fields.

settings.title: Env Vars
settings.description: Secret environment variable names to unset for sandboxed commands

settings.title: Fallback Model
settings.description: Fallback model chain tried in order when the primary model is overloaded, unavailable, or returns a non-retryable server error. Capped at three entries after deduplication; accepts model names or aliases, and "default" expands to the account-type default. Overridden by the --fallback-model CLI flag for the session. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/model-config#fallback-model-chains

settings.title: Fast Mode
settings.description: Enable fast mode, which uses Claude Opus 4.8 by default (since v2.1.154) for faster output at higher per-token cost without downgrading to a smaller model. Requires extra usage enabled. Toggle with the /fast command. Available on Opus 4.8 and Opus 4.7 (Opus 4.7 fast mode deprecated June 2026). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/fast-mode

settings.title: Files
settings.description: Credential file paths to hide from sandboxed commands

settings.title: Force Login Method
settings.description: Force a specific login method: "claudeai" for Claude Pro/Max, "console" for Console billing

settings.title: Force Login Org UUID
settings.description: Organization UUID to use for OAuth login

settings.title: FORCE PROMPT CACHING 5 M
settings.description: Force a 5-minute cache TTL regardless of authentication method; overrides ENABLE_PROMPT_CACHING_1H or a managed-settings TTL. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/prompt-caching#override-the-ttl

settings.title: Gcp Auth Refresh
settings.description: Command to run when GCP credentials are expired or cannot be loaded. The command's output is displayed to the user but interactive input is not supported; it times out after three minutes. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/google-vertex-ai#advanced-credential-configuration
settings.writeReason: Executable callbacks require a dedicated reviewed workflow.

settings.title: GOOGLE APPLICATION CREDENTIALS
settings.description: Path to a GCP credential configuration file (service account key or workload identity federation config) used for Vertex AI authentication. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/google-vertex-ai#3-configure-gcp-credentials
settings.writeReason: Authentication and secret values remain provider-owned.

settings.title: Include Co Authored By
settings.description: DEPRECATED. Use 'attribution' instead. Whether to include the co-authored-by Claude byline in git commits and pull requests (default: true)

settings.title: MAX MCP OUTPUT TOKENS
settings.description: Maximum number of tokens allowed in MCP tool output before truncation (default: 25000). Claude Code displays a warning above 10000 tokens. For tools that declare anthropic/maxResultSizeChars, that character limit replaces this token limit for text content, but image content from those tools is still subject to this limit. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp

settings.title: MAX THINKING TOKENS
settings.description: Override the extended thinking token budget; set to 0 to disable thinking on the Anthropic API. On adaptive reasoning models (Opus 4.7+, Opus 4.8, Fable 5) a nonzero budget is ignored unless CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_ADAPTIVE_THINKING is set. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/model-config#extended-thinking

settings.title: MCP CLIENT SECRET
settings.description: OAuth client secret for MCP servers that require pre-configured credentials (avoids the interactive prompt when adding a server with --client-secret). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars
settings.writeReason: Authentication and secret values remain provider-owned.

settings.title: MCP OAUTH CALLBACK PORT
settings.description: Fixed port for the OAuth redirect callback, as an alternative to --callback-port when adding an MCP server with pre-configured credentials. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: OTEL EXPORTER OTLP CERTIFICATE
settings.description: Path to the CA certificate for gRPC OTLP mTLS. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/monitoring-usage#mtls-authentication

settings.title: OTEL EXPORTER OTLP CLIENT CERTIFICATE
settings.description: Path to the client certificate for gRPC OTLP mTLS. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/monitoring-usage#mtls-authentication

settings.title: OTEL EXPORTER OTLP CLIENT KEY
settings.description: Path to the client private key for gRPC OTLP mTLS. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/monitoring-usage#mtls-authentication
settings.writeReason: Authentication and secret values remain provider-owned.

settings.title: OTEL EXPORTER OTLP HEADERS
settings.description: Headers sent with OTLP exporter requests (e.g. Authorization=Bearer token). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/monitoring-usage#common-configuration-variables
settings.writeReason: Authentication and secret values remain provider-owned.

settings.title: OTEL METRICS INCLUDE ACCOUNT UUID
settings.description: Include the user.account_uuid and user.account_id attributes on metrics (default true). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/monitoring-usage#metrics-cardinality-control

settings.title: Stop Failure
settings.description: Hooks that run when a turn ends due to an API error (e.g., rate_limit, authentication_failed, billing_error, invalid_request, server_error, max_output_tokens, unknown). Matcher can scope to specific error types. Hook output and exit code are ignored. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks
settings.writeReason: This structured value contains provider-owned authentication fields.

settings.title: Voice Enabled
settings.description: Legacy alias for voice.enabled; prefer the voice object. Requires a Claude.ai account. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings#available-settings

settings.title: Auto Compact Enabled
settings.description: Automatically compact the conversation when context approaches the limit. Also configurable via the DISABLE_AUTO_COMPACT environment variable. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings#available-settings

settings.title: Auto Memory Directory
settings.description: Custom directory path for auto-memory storage. Supports ~/ prefix for home-directory expansion. Ignored if set in checked-in project settings (.claude/settings.json) for security. Defaults to ~/.claude/projects/<sanitized-cwd>/memory/ when unset. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/memory

settings.title: Auto Memory Enabled
settings.description: Enable automatic memory saves that capture useful context to .claude/memory/. Also configurable via CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_AUTO_MEMORY environment variable (set to 1 to disable, 0 to enable). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/memory#auto-memory

settings.title: Auto Submit
settings.description: In hold mode, auto-submit the prompt on key release when the transcript is at least three words long

settings.title: Away Summary Enabled
settings.description: Show a one-line session recap when you return to the terminal after a few minutes away. Set to false (or turn off Session recap in /config) to disable. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings#available-settings

settings.title: Bg Isolation
settings.description: Isolation mode for background sessions. "worktree" blocks Edit/Write in main checkout until EnterWorktree is called; "none" lets background jobs edit the working copy directly without EnterWorktree, for repos where worktrees are impractical. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings#worktree-settings

settings.title: CLAUDE AUTOCOMPACT PCT OVERRIDE
settings.description: Context capacity percentage threshold for auto-compaction (1-100)

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE ADDITIONAL DIRECTORIES CLAUDE MD
settings.description: Load CLAUDE.md memory files from additional directories

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE DISABLE AUTO MEMORY
settings.description: Disable automatic memory feature

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE DISABLE BG SHELL PRESSURE REAP
settings.description: UNDOCUMENTED. Disable automatic memory-pressure reaping of idle background shell commands (added v2.1.193).

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE DISABLE CLAUDE MDS
settings.description: Prevent loading CLAUDE.md memory files

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE DISABLE FEEDBACK SURVEY
settings.description: Disable session quality feedback surveys

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE ENABLE AWAY SUMMARY
settings.description: Override session recap/away summary availability

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE REMOTE
settings.description: Indicates a remote web environment session

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE REMOTE SESSION ID
settings.description: Cloud session identifier for remote sessions

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE RESUME PROMPT
settings.description: Override the continuation message injected when resuming a session that ended mid-turn (default "Continue from where you left off."). An empty string uses the default. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE SESSIONEND HOOKS TIMEOUT MS
settings.description: Override the time budget in milliseconds for SessionEnd hooks (default 1500, raised up to 60000 by the highest configured per-hook timeout). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE SKIP PROMPT HISTORY
settings.description: Disable transcript writes entirely. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings#environment-variables

settings.title: CLAUDE REMOTE CONTROL SESSION NAME PREFIX
settings.description: Prefix for auto-generated Remote Control session names when no explicit name is set. Defaults to the machine hostname, producing names like myhost-graceful-unicorn. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/remote-control#start-a-remote-control-session

settings.title: Command
settings.description: A shell command or path to a script that displays session information (context usage, costs, git status, etc.) by reading JSON data from stdin and writing output to stdout. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/statusline
settings.writeReason: Workspace owns this launch overlay for the live context meter.

settings.title: DEBUG
settings.description: Set to a truthy value (1, true, yes, or on) to enable debug mode, equivalent to --debug. Logs are written to ~/.claude/debug/<session-id>.txt. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: DISABLE AUTO COMPACT
settings.description: Set to 1 to disable automatic compaction when approaching the context limit. The manual /compact command remains available. Equivalent to autoCompactEnabled: false. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: DISABLE COMPACT
settings.description: Set to 1 to disable all compaction: both automatic compaction and the manual /compact command. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: Disable Remote Control
settings.description: Disable Remote Control: blocks claude remote-control, the --remote-control flag, auto-start, and the in-session toggle. Requires Claude Code v2.1.128 or later. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/remote-control

settings.title: ENABLE PROMPT CACHING 1 H
settings.description: Request a 1-hour prompt cache TTL instead of the 5-minute default (billed at a higher rate). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/prompt-caching#cache-lifetime

settings.title: Environment
settings.description: Entries for the auto mode classifier environment section. Replaces the built-in environment context entirely unless the literal string "$defaults" is included as an entry, which splices the built-in defaults in at that position.

settings.title: Fast Mode Per Session Opt In
settings.description: Require per-session opt-in for fast mode. When true, fast mode does not persist across sessions and users must enable it with /fast each session. Useful for controlling costs. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/fast-mode

settings.title: Feedback Survey Rate
settings.description: Probability (0–1) that the session quality survey appears when eligible. A value of 0.05 means 5% of eligible sessions. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings#available-settings

settings.title: Force Remote Settings Refresh
settings.description: (Managed settings only) Block CLI startup until remote managed settings are freshly fetched from the server. If the fetch fails, the CLI exits (fail-closed) rather than continuing with cached settings. When not set, startup continues without waiting for remote settings. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/server-managed-settings

settings.title: Instructions Loaded
settings.description: Hooks that run when a CLAUDE.md or .claude/rules/*.md file is loaded into context. Fires at session start and when files are lazily loaded (e.g., nested traversal, path glob match). No decision control; used for audit logging and observability. Does not support matchers. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks#instructionsloaded
settings.writeReason: This structured value contains provider-owned authentication fields.

settings.title: Language
settings.description: Configure Claude's preferred response language (e.g., "japanese", "spanish", "french"). Claude will respond in this language by default. Also sets the voice dictation language and terminal tab session title generation. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings#available-settings

settings.title: OTEL METRICS INCLUDE SESSION ID
settings.description: Include the session.id attribute on metrics (default true). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/monitoring-usage#metrics-cardinality-control

settings.title: Post Compact
settings.description: Hooks that run after the context is compacted. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks
settings.writeReason: This structured value contains provider-owned authentication fields.

settings.title: Pre Compact
settings.description: Hooks that run before the context is compacted

settings.title: Effort Level
settings.description: Persist adaptive reasoning effort across sessions. Set to low, medium, high, or xhigh; max and ultracode are session-only and are not accepted in the settings file (use the CLAUDE_CODE_EFFORT_LEVEL environment variable or /effort for a session-only max). Effort is supported on Fable 5, Opus 4.8, Opus 4.7, Opus 4.6, and Sonnet 4.6, with xhigh sitting between high and max (added in v2.1.111). Defaults: Fable 5 and Opus 4.8 default to high; Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 default to high on all plans (Pro/Max raised from medium to high in v2.1.117); Opus 4.7 defaults to xhigh. Use /effort auto to reset to model default. Also configurable via CLAUDE_CODE_EFFORT_LEVEL environment variable. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/model-config#adjust-effort-level

settings.title: ENABLE TOOL SEARCH
settings.description: Control MCP tool search: "true" always defers and sends the beta header (requests fail on Vertex AI models earlier than Sonnet 4.5/Opus 4.5 or on proxies that do not support tool_reference); "auto" loads tools upfront if they fit within 10% of context; "auto:N" sets a custom threshold percentage (e.g. auto:5); "false" loads all tools upfront. Also applies when ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL points to a non-first-party host. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/google-vertex-ai#4-configure-claude-code

settings.title: FALLBACK FOR ALL PRIMARY MODELS
settings.description: Set to any non-empty value to make all models (not only Opus) stop retrying with a repeated-overload error when no fallback model is configured. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: MAX STRUCTURED OUTPUT RETRIES
settings.description: Number of times to retry when the model's response fails validation against --json-schema in non-interactive (-p) mode (default 5). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: Model
settings.description: Override the default model used by Claude Code. For finer control, use environment variables: ANTHROPIC_MODEL (runtime override), ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_OPUS_MODEL, ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL, ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL (per-class pinning), CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL (subagent model). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/model-config

settings.title: Model Overrides
settings.description: Map Anthropic model IDs to provider-specific model IDs such as Bedrock inference profile ARNs, Vertex AI version names, or Foundry deployment names. Each model picker entry uses its mapped value when calling the provider API. Unknown keys are ignored. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/model-config#override-model-ids-per-version
settings.writeReason: Named entries need a preservation-safe dedicated editor.

settings.title: Model Overrides entry
settings.description: Configure modelOverrides.*.
settings.writeReason: Add and edit entries through the parent structured map control.

settings.title: Post Tool Batch
settings.description: Hooks that run after a full batch of parallel tool calls resolves, before the next model call. Exit code 2 blocks the agentic loop. Does not support matchers. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks
settings.writeReason: This structured value contains provider-owned authentication fields.

settings.title: Show Thinking Summaries
settings.description: Show thinking summaries in the transcript view (Ctrl+O). Thinking summaries are not generated by default in interactive sessions; set to true to restore. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings#available-settings

settings.title: Skill Listing Budget Fraction
settings.description: Fraction of the model context window reserved for the skill listing sent to Claude (default 0.01 = 1%). When the listing exceeds this, descriptions are shortened to fit. Also configurable via the SLASH_COMMAND_TOOL_CHAR_BUDGET environment variable (fixed character count). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills#skill-descriptions-are-cut-short

settings.title: ANTHROPIC AWS BASE URL
settings.description: Override the Claude Platform on AWS endpoint URL. Default is https://aws-external-anthropic.{region}.api.aws. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/claude-platform-on-aws#route-through-a-corporate-proxy

settings.title: ANTHROPIC AWS WORKSPACE ID
settings.description: Required workspace ID for Claude Platform on AWS; sent as the anthropic-workspace-id header on every request. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/claude-platform-on-aws#2-configure-claude-code

settings.title: ANTHROPIC BASE URL
settings.description: Override API endpoint URL for proxy or gateway routing

settings.title: ANTHROPIC BEDROCK BASE URL
settings.description: Override Amazon Bedrock endpoint URL

settings.title: ANTHROPIC BEDROCK MANTLE BASE URL
settings.description: Override Bedrock Mantle endpoint URL

settings.title: ANTHROPIC BEDROCK SERVICE TIER
settings.description: Select Bedrock service tier; sent as X-Amzn-Bedrock-Service-Tier header. See https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md#21122

settings.title: ANTHROPIC CUSTOM HEADERS
settings.description: Custom HTTP headers for API requests (newline-separated 'Name: Value' pairs)
settings.writeReason: Authentication and secret values remain provider-owned.

settings.title: ANTHROPIC FOUNDRY BASE URL
settings.description: Microsoft Foundry resource URL

settings.title: ANTHROPIC VERTEX BASE URL
settings.description: Override Google Vertex AI endpoint URL

settings.title: API FORCE IDLE TIMEOUT
settings.description: Override the 5-minute idle timeout for streaming responses (0 disables it). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: AWS REGION
settings.description: AWS region for Amazon Bedrock and Claude Platform on AWS requests (e.g. us-east-1). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/amazon-bedrock#3-configure-claude-code

settings.title: Ax Screen Reader
settings.description: Render screen-reader-friendly output: flat text without decorative borders or animations screen-reader mode always uses the classic renderer, so the tui setting has no effect while it is active. The CLAUDE_AX_SCREEN_READER environment variable and --ax-screen-reader flag take precedence. Requires Claude Code v2.1.181 or later. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings#available-settings

settings.title: Base Ref
settings.description: Whether to branch worktrees from origin/<default> (fresh) or local HEAD (head). Default: fresh. Set to 'head' to preserve unpushed commits in new worktrees. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings#worktree-settings

settings.title: CLAUDE AX SCREEN READER
settings.description: Set to 1 to render screen-reader friendly output: flat text without decorative borders or animations. Set to 0 to force screen-reader mode off even when axScreenReader is true. The --ax-screen-reader flag takes precedence. Requires Claude Code v2.1.181 or later. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE ALT SCREEN FULL REPAINT
settings.description: Force a full-screen repaint on every frame in fullscreen mode. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE ARTIFACT AUTO OPEN
settings.description: Set to 0 to stop auto-opening the browser when a new artifact is created. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/artifacts#create-an-artifact

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE CLIENT CERT
settings.description: Client certificate file path for mutual TLS

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE CLIENT KEY
settings.description: Client private key file path for mutual TLS
settings.writeReason: Authentication and secret values remain provider-owned.

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE DISABLE EXPERIMENTAL BETAS
settings.description: Strip anthropic-beta headers from API requests. See https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md#21123

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE DISABLE WORKFLOWS
settings.description: Set to 1 to disable workflows. Equivalent to the disableWorkflows setting. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE ENABLE AUTO MODE
settings.description: Set to 1 to make auto mode available on Amazon Bedrock, Google Cloud Vertex AI, and Microsoft Foundry (requires v2.1.158+; no effect on the Anthropic API where auto mode is available by default). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE ENHANCED TELEMETRY BETA
settings.description: Enable the enhanced telemetry (tracing) beta. ENABLE_ENHANCED_TELEMETRY_BETA is also accepted. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/monitoring-usage#traces-beta

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE HIDE CWD
settings.description: Hide the working directory in the startup logo. See https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md#21126

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE OPUS 4 6 FAST MODE OVERRIDE
settings.description: Set to 1 to pin fast mode to Claude Opus 4.6 instead of the default Opus 4.8 (the fast-mode default since v2.1.154). With this set, /fast runs on Opus 4.6. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/fast-mode

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE OTEL DIAG STDERR
settings.description: Set to 1 to write OpenTelemetry exporter diagnostic errors to stderr (otherwise shown only with --debug). Requires v2.1.179+. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE PROPAGATE TRACEPARENT
settings.description: Propagate the W3C traceparent header on API requests when using a custom ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/monitoring-usage#traces-beta

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE PROXY RESOLVES HOSTS
settings.description: Allow proxy to handle DNS resolution

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE USE ANTHROPIC AWS
settings.description: Enable Claude Platform on AWS as the API provider. Bedrock and Foundry take precedence if also set. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/claude-platform-on-aws#2-configure-claude-code

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE USE BEDROCK
settings.description: Enable Amazon Bedrock as the API provider. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/amazon-bedrock#3-configure-claude-code

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE USE FOUNDRY
settings.description: Enable Microsoft Foundry as the API provider. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/microsoft-foundry#3-configure-claude-code

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE USE MANTLE
settings.description: Enable the Mantle endpoint (native Anthropic API shape on Bedrock). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/amazon-bedrock#enable-mantle

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE USE VERTEX
settings.description: Enable Google Vertex AI as the API provider. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/google-vertex-ai#4-configure-claude-code

settings.title: CLAUDE ENABLE BYTE WATCHDOG
settings.description: Set to 1 to force-enable, or 0 to force-disable, the byte-level streaming idle watchdog that aborts a connection when no bytes arrive within the configured timeout. Enabled by default on direct Anthropic API and Claude Platform on AWS connections. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: CLAUDE ENABLE BYTE WATCHDOG BEDROCK
settings.description: Set to 1 to enable the byte-level streaming idle watchdog on Amazon Bedrock eventstream responses (off by default). Configure the timeout with CLAUDE_STREAM_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: CLAUDE ENABLE STREAM WATCHDOG
settings.description: Set to 1 to force-enable, or 0 to force-disable, the event-level streaming idle watchdog. When unset, server-controlled on the direct Anthropic API and off on other providers. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: CLAUDE STREAM IDLE TIMEOUT MS
settings.description: Timeout in milliseconds before the streaming idle watchdog closes a stalled connection. When set explicitly the minimum is 300000 (5 minutes); lower values are clamped. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: CLOUD ML REGION
settings.description: Vertex AI region: global, a multi-region location (eu, us), or a specific region (e.g. us-east5). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/google-vertex-ai#region-configuration

settings.title: DISABLE AUTOUPDATER
settings.description: Set to 1 to disable automatic background updates. Manual claude update still works; use DISABLE_UPDATES to block both. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: DISABLE COST WARNINGS
settings.description: Set to 1 to disable cost warning messages. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: Disable Deep Link Registration
settings.description: Set to "disable" to prevent Claude Code from registering the `claude://` deep-link protocol handler on startup. Most useful in managed settings where users cannot override it. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings#available-settings

settings.title: DISABLE GROWTHBOOK
settings.description: Set to 1 to disable GrowthBook feature-flag fetching and use code defaults for every flag. Telemetry stays on unless DISABLE_TELEMETRY is also set. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: DISABLE INSTALLATION CHECKS
settings.description: Set to 1 to disable installation warnings (use only when manually managing the installation location). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: DISABLE TELEMETRY
settings.description: Set to 1 to opt out of telemetry. Also disables feature-flag fetching (same effect as DISABLE_GROWTHBOOK). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: File Checkpointing Enabled
settings.description: Snapshot edited files so /rewind can restore them. Also configurable via the CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_FILE_CHECKPOINTING environment variable (set to 1 to disable). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/checkpointing

settings.title: HTTP PROXY
settings.description: HTTP proxy URL

settings.title: HTTPS PROXY
settings.description: HTTPS proxy URL (recommended over HTTP_PROXY)

settings.title: Include Git Instructions
settings.description: Include built-in git commit and PR workflow instructions in Claude's system prompt. Also configurable via CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_GIT_INSTRUCTIONS environment variable (set to 1 to disable). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings#available-settings

settings.title: IS DEMO
settings.description: Set to 1 to enable demo mode: hides email and organization name from the header and /status output and skips onboarding. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: Minimum Version
settings.description: Minimum Claude Code version to stay on. Prevents downgrades when switching release channels. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings#available-settings

settings.title: NO PROXY
settings.description: Domains to bypass proxy (space or comma-separated, or '*' for all)

settings.title: OTEL EXPORTER OTLP ENDPOINT
settings.description: OTLP exporter endpoint for all signals (e.g. http://localhost:4317). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/monitoring-usage#common-configuration-variables

settings.title: OTEL EXPORTER OTLP LOGS ENDPOINT
settings.description: OTLP exporter endpoint override for logs. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/monitoring-usage#common-configuration-variables

settings.title: OTEL EXPORTER OTLP LOGS PROTOCOL
settings.description: OTLP protocol override for logs: grpc, http/json, or http/protobuf. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/monitoring-usage#common-configuration-variables

settings.title: OTEL EXPORTER OTLP METRICS ENDPOINT
settings.description: OTLP exporter endpoint override for metrics. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/monitoring-usage#common-configuration-variables

settings.title: OTEL EXPORTER OTLP METRICS PROTOCOL
settings.description: OTLP protocol override for metrics: grpc, http/json, or http/protobuf. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/monitoring-usage#common-configuration-variables

settings.title: OTEL EXPORTER OTLP METRICS TEMPORALITY PREFERENCE
settings.description: Metrics temporality preference: delta (default) or cumulative. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/monitoring-usage#common-configuration-variables

settings.title: OTEL EXPORTER OTLP PROTOCOL
settings.description: OTLP exporter protocol for all signals: grpc, http/json, or http/protobuf. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/monitoring-usage#common-configuration-variables

settings.title: OTEL EXPORTER OTLP TRACES ENDPOINT
settings.description: OTLP exporter endpoint override for traces. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/monitoring-usage#traces-beta

settings.title: OTEL EXPORTER OTLP TRACES PROTOCOL
settings.description: OTLP protocol override for traces: grpc, http/json, or http/protobuf. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/monitoring-usage#traces-beta

settings.title: OTEL LOG RAW API BODIES
settings.description: Log raw API request/response bodies. Set to 1, or to file:<dir> to write them to a directory. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/monitoring-usage#common-configuration-variables

settings.title: OTEL LOG USER PROMPTS
settings.description: Include user prompt text in OpenTelemetry log events (disabled by default). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/monitoring-usage#common-configuration-variables

settings.title: OTEL LOGS EXPORT INTERVAL
settings.description: Logs export interval in milliseconds (default 5000). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/monitoring-usage#common-configuration-variables

settings.title: OTEL LOGS EXPORTER
settings.description: OpenTelemetry logs exporter(s) as a comma-separated list. Valid values: otlp, console, none. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/monitoring-usage#common-configuration-variables

settings.title: OTEL METRIC EXPORT INTERVAL
settings.description: Metrics export interval in milliseconds (default 60000). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/monitoring-usage#common-configuration-variables

settings.title: OTEL METRICS EXPORTER
settings.description: OpenTelemetry metrics exporter(s) as a comma-separated list. Valid values: otlp, prometheus, console, none. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/monitoring-usage#common-configuration-variables

settings.title: OTEL METRICS INCLUDE ENTRYPOINT
settings.description: Include the app.entrypoint attribute on metrics (default false). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/monitoring-usage#metrics-cardinality-control

settings.title: OTEL METRICS INCLUDE RESOURCE ATTRIBUTES
settings.description: Include configured OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES on metric data points (default true). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/monitoring-usage#metrics-cardinality-control

settings.title: OTEL METRICS INCLUDE VERSION
settings.description: Include the app.version attribute on metrics (default false). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/monitoring-usage#metrics-cardinality-control

settings.title: OTEL RESOURCE ATTRIBUTES
settings.description: Comma-separated key=value resource attributes attached to all telemetry. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/monitoring-usage#multi-team-organization-support

settings.title: OTEL TRACES EXPORT INTERVAL
settings.description: Traces export interval in milliseconds (default 5000). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/monitoring-usage#traces-beta

settings.title: OTEL TRACES EXPORTER
settings.description: OpenTelemetry traces exporter(s) as a comma-separated list. Valid values: otlp, console, none. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/monitoring-usage#traces-beta

settings.title: Output Style
settings.description: Controls the output style for assistant responses. Built-in styles: default, Explanatory, Learning. Custom styles can be added in ~/.claude/output-styles/ or .claude/output-styles/. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/output-styles

settings.title: Required Maximum Version
settings.description: (Managed settings only) Maximum Claude Code version allowed to start. If the running version is newer, Claude Code exits at startup with instructions to install an approved version. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/admin-setup#decide-what-to-enforce

settings.title: Required Minimum Version
settings.description: (Managed settings only) Minimum Claude Code version required to start. If the running version is older, Claude Code exits at startup rather than only warning. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/admin-setup#decide-what-to-enforce

settings.title: Skip Web Fetch Preflight
settings.description: Skip the WebFetch blocklist check for enterprise environments with restrictive security policies. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings#available-settings

settings.title: Sparse Paths
settings.description: Directories to check out in each worktree via git sparse-checkout (cone mode). Only the listed paths are written to disk, which is faster in large monorepos. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings#worktree-settings

settings.title: Symlink Directories
settings.description: Directories to symlink from the main repository into each worktree to avoid duplicating large directories on disk. No directories are symlinked by default. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings#worktree-settings

settings.title: Syntax Highlighting Disabled
settings.description: Disable syntax highlighting in diffs, code blocks, and file previews. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings#available-settings

settings.title: VERTEX REGION CLAUDE 3 5 HAIKU
settings.description: Override the Vertex AI region for Claude 3.5 Haiku (used when CLOUD_ML_REGION=global). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: VERTEX REGION CLAUDE 3 5 SONNET
settings.description: Override the Vertex AI region for Claude 3.5 Sonnet (used when CLOUD_ML_REGION=global). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: VERTEX REGION CLAUDE 3 7 SONNET
settings.description: Override the Vertex AI region for Claude 3.7 Sonnet (used when CLOUD_ML_REGION=global). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: VERTEX REGION CLAUDE 4 0 OPUS
settings.description: Override the Vertex AI region for Claude 4.0 Opus (used when CLOUD_ML_REGION=global). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: VERTEX REGION CLAUDE 4 0 SONNET
settings.description: Override the Vertex AI region for Claude 4.0 Sonnet (used when CLOUD_ML_REGION=global). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: VERTEX REGION CLAUDE 4 1 OPUS
settings.description: Override the Vertex AI region for Claude 4.1 Opus (used when CLOUD_ML_REGION=global). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: VERTEX REGION CLAUDE 4 5 OPUS
settings.description: Override the Vertex AI region for Claude Opus 4.5 (used when CLOUD_ML_REGION=global). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: VERTEX REGION CLAUDE 4 5 SONNET
settings.description: Override the Vertex AI region for Claude Sonnet 4.5 (used when CLOUD_ML_REGION=global). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: VERTEX REGION CLAUDE 4 6 OPUS
settings.description: Override the Vertex AI region for Claude Opus 4.6 (used when CLOUD_ML_REGION=global). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: VERTEX REGION CLAUDE 4 6 SONNET
settings.description: Override the Vertex AI region for Claude Sonnet 4.6 (used when CLOUD_ML_REGION=global). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: VERTEX REGION CLAUDE 4 7 OPUS
settings.description: Override the Vertex AI region for Claude Opus 4.7 (used when CLOUD_ML_REGION=global). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: VERTEX REGION CLAUDE 4 8 OPUS
settings.description: Override the Vertex AI region for Claude Opus 4.8 (used when CLOUD_ML_REGION=global). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: VERTEX REGION CLAUDE FABLE 5
settings.description: Override the Vertex AI region for Claude Fable 5 (used when CLOUD_ML_REGION=global). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: VERTEX REGION CLAUDE HAIKU 4 5
settings.description: Override the Vertex AI region for Claude Haiku 4.5 (used when CLOUD_ML_REGION=global). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: Wheel Scroll Acceleration Enabled
settings.description: In fullscreen rendering, accelerate mouse-wheel scroll speed during fast scrolls (v2.1.174+). Set to false for a constant scroll rate per wheel notch. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/fullscreen#mouse-wheel-scrolling

settings.title: Workflow Keyword Trigger Enabled
settings.description: Whether including the keyword "ultracode" in a prompt triggers a dynamic workflow. Requires Claude Code v2.1.157 or later. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings#available-settings

settings.title: BETA TRACING ENDPOINT
settings.description: Endpoint that, together with ENABLE_BETA_TRACING_DETAILED=1, activates detailed beta tracing spans (e.g. claude_code.hook). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/monitoring-usage#traces-beta

settings.title: CLAUDE CLIENT PRESENCE FILE
settings.description: Path to a file whose existence marks the user as present; while it exists, mobile push notifications are skipped (v2.1.181+). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE STOP HOOK BLOCK CAP
settings.description: Override the default maximum consecutive Stop hook blocks (default: 8) before the turn ends with a warning. Raise this when a stop hook legitimately needs more than 8 iterations to converge. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide

settings.title: CLAUDE PROJECT DIR
settings.description: Project root directory path (also provided to hooks)

settings.title: Cwd Changed
settings.description: Hooks that run when the working directory changes. Provides cwd (new directory) and previous_cwd. Matchers are ignored; fires on every directory change. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks#cwdchanged
settings.writeReason: This structured value contains provider-owned authentication fields.

settings.title: Disable All Hooks
settings.description: Disable all hooks and statusLine execution. When true in managed settings, user and project-level disableAllHooks cannot override it. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks#disable-or-remove-hooks

settings.title: ENABLE BETA TRACING DETAILED
settings.description: Emit detailed spans including hook execution when the tracing beta is enabled. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/monitoring-usage#traces-beta

settings.title: File Changed
settings.description: Hooks that run when a watched file is created, modified, or deleted. Supports filename matchers. Provides file_path and file_event_type (created, modified, deleted). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks#filechanged
settings.writeReason: This structured value contains provider-owned authentication fields.

settings.title: Hide Vim Mode Indicator
settings.description: Set to true when your status line script renders the vim mode indicator itself, to suppress the built-in vim mode display. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/statusline#manually-configure-a-status-line
settings.writeReason: Workspace owns this launch overlay for the live context meter.

settings.title: Message Display
settings.description: Hooks that run while assistant message text is displayed
settings.writeReason: This structured value contains provider-owned authentication fields.

settings.title: Notification
settings.description: Hooks that trigger on notifications
settings.writeReason: This structured value contains provider-owned authentication fields.

settings.title: Padding
settings.description: Optional number of extra horizontal spacing characters added to the status line content; defaults to 0.
settings.writeReason: Workspace owns this launch overlay for the live context meter.

settings.title: Setup
settings.description: UNDOCUMENTED. Hooks that run during repository initialization (--init, --init-only) or maintenance (--maintenance)
settings.writeReason: This structured value contains provider-owned authentication fields.

settings.title: User Prompt Expansion
settings.description: Hooks that run when a user-typed command expands into a prompt, before it reaches Claude. Exit code 2 blocks the expansion. Supports matchers on command name. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks
settings.writeReason: This structured value contains provider-owned authentication fields.

settings.title: User Prompt Submit
settings.description: Hooks that run when a user submits a prompt
settings.writeReason: This structured value contains provider-owned authentication fields.

settings.title: Additional Directories
settings.description: Additional directories to include in the permission scope

settings.title: Advisor Model
settings.description: Model for the server-side advisor tool. Accepts a model alias ("opus", "sonnet", or "fable" on v2.1.170+) or a full model ID. Overridden by the --advisor CLI flag for the session, and blocked when the model is outside the availableModels allowlist. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/advisor

settings.title: Allow
settings.description: List of permission rules for allowed operations

settings.title: Allow Managed Permission Rules Only
settings.description: (Managed settings only) Prevent user and project settings from defining allow, ask, or deny permission rules. Only rules in managed settings apply. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings#permission-settings

settings.title: Allow Unsandboxed Commands
settings.description: Allow commands to run outside the sandbox via the dangerouslyDisableSandbox parameter. When false, the dangerouslyDisableSandbox parameter is completely ignored and all commands must run sandboxed. Default: true. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/sandboxing#limitations

settings.title: Allowed Channel Plugins
settings.description: (Managed settings only) Allowlist of channel plugins that may run, each identified by its marketplace and plugin name. When set, only the listed plugins can push inbound channel messages while channelsEnabled is true. Replaces the default Anthropic allowlist when set; undefined falls back to the default and an empty array blocks all channel plugins. Requires channelsEnabled: true. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/channels#restrict-which-channel-plugins-can-run

settings.title: Allowed Domains
settings.description: Allowlist of network domains for sandboxed commands. Supports wildcard patterns like *.example.com. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/sandboxing#network-isolation

settings.title: Allowed Http Hook Urls
settings.description: Allowlist of URL patterns that HTTP hooks may target. Supports * as a wildcard. When set, hooks with non-matching URLs are blocked. Undefined = no restriction, empty array = block all HTTP hooks. Arrays merge across settings sources. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings#hook-configuration

settings.title: Allowed Mcp Servers
settings.description: Enterprise allowlist of MCP servers that can be used. Applies to all scopes including enterprise servers from managed-mcp.json. If undefined, all servers are allowed. If empty array, no servers are allowed. Denylist takes precedence - if a server is on both lists, it is denied. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp#restriction-options

settings.title: Ask
settings.description: List of permission rules that should always prompt for confirmation

settings.title: Auto Scroll Enabled
settings.description: In fullscreen rendering, follow new output to the bottom of the conversation. Appears in /config as Auto-scroll. Permission prompts still scroll into view when this is off. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/fullscreen

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE DISABLE POLICY SKILLS
settings.description: Skip loading system-wide policy skills

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE MCP ALLOWLIST ENV
settings.description: Isolate MCP server environments to allowlisted variables

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE POWERSHELL RESPECT EXECUTION POLICY
settings.description: Set to 1 to stop Claude Code from passing -ExecutionPolicy Bypass when spawning PowerShell for tool calls, hooks, and status line commands. By default Claude Code bypasses execution policy so .ps1 scripts work on default-Restricted Windows installs. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE SAFE MODE
settings.description: Set to 1 to start in safe mode: CLAUDE.md, skills, plugins, hooks, MCP servers, custom commands and agents, output styles, workflows, custom themes, custom keybindings, status line and file-suggestion commands, LSP servers, and auto-memory do not load, for troubleshooting a broken configuration. Managed settings policy still applies. Equivalent to passing --safe-mode; directly spawned child processes inherit the variable. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

settings.title: Claude Md
settings.description: (Managed settings only) CLAUDE.md-style instructions injected as organization-managed memory. Honored only from managed/policy settings. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings#available-settings

settings.title: Claude Md Excludes
settings.description: Glob patterns for CLAUDE.md files to exclude from loading. Useful in monorepos to skip irrelevant instructions from other teams. Patterns match against absolute file paths. Arrays merge across settings layers. Managed policy CLAUDE.md files cannot be excluded. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/memory#exclude-specific-claude-md-files

settings.title: Config Change
settings.description: Hooks that run when settings, managed settings, or skill files change during a session. Supports matchers: user_settings, project_settings, local_settings, policy_settings, skills. Command handlers only. Exit code 2 blocks the change (except policy_settings which is audit-only). See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks#configchange
settings.writeReason: This structured value contains provider-owned authentication fields.

settings.title: Default Mode
settings.description: Default permission mode. "default": prompts on first use. "acceptEdits": auto-accepts file edits. "plan": read-only, no modifications. UNDOCUMENTED. "delegate": coordination-only for agent team leads (agent teams are experimental; enable via CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS). "dontAsk": auto-denies unless pre-approved via permissions. "bypassPermissions": skips all prompts (use only in isolated environments). "auto": auto-approves tool calls with background safety checks that verify actions align with your request. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/permissions

settings.title: Denied Mcp Servers
settings.description: Enterprise denylist of MCP servers that are explicitly blocked. If a server is on the denylist, it will be blocked across all scopes including enterprise. Denylist takes precedence over allowlist - if a server is on both lists, it is denied. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp#restriction-options

settings.title: Deny
settings.description: List of permission rules for denied operations

settings.title: Disable Auto Mode
settings.description: Set to "disable" to prevent auto mode from being activated: removes auto from the Shift+Tab cycle and rejects --permission-mode auto at startup. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings#available-settings

settings.title: Disable Auto Mode
settings.description: Set to "disable" to prevent auto mode from being activated. Removes `auto` from the Shift+Tab cycle and rejects `--permission-mode auto` at startup. Most useful in managed settings where users cannot override it. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/permissions

settings.title: Disable Bypass Permissions Mode
settings.description: Disable the ability to bypass permission prompts

settings.title: Disable Skill Shell Execution
settings.description: Disable inline shell execution for `` !`...` `` and ` ```! ` blocks in skills and custom slash commands from user, project, plugin, or additional-directory sources. Commands are replaced with [shell command execution disabled by policy] instead of being run. Bundled and managed skills are not affected. Most useful in managed settings where users cannot override it. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings#available-settings

settings.title: Enable Weaker Network Isolation
settings.description: macOS only. Allow access to the system TLS trust service (com.apple.trustd.agent) in the sandbox. Required for Go-based tools like gh, gcloud, and terraform to verify TLS certificates when using httpProxyPort with a MITM proxy and custom CA. Reduces security by opening a potential data exfiltration path. Default: false. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings#sandbox-settings

settings.title: Enabled Platforms
settings.description: Limit the entire sandbox configuration to the listed platforms. On platforms not in the list the sandbox config is inert: no sandbox, no auto-allow, no startup warning, and no failIfUnavailable exit. When omitted, all supported platforms are included. Only honored from managed (policy) settings.

settings.title: Hard deny
settings.description: Rules for the auto mode classifier hard-deny section. Hard-deny rules block unconditionally regardless of user intent. Replaces the built-in hard-deny rules entirely unless the literal string "$defaults" is included as an entry, which splices the built-in defaults in at that position. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/permissions

settings.title: Http Hook Allowed Env Vars
settings.description: Allowlist of environment variable names HTTP hooks may interpolate into headers. When set, each hook's effective allowedEnvVars is the intersection with this list. Undefined = no restriction. Arrays merge across settings sources. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings#hook-configuration

settings.title: Input Needed Notif Enabled
settings.description: When Remote Control is connected, send a push notification to your phone when a permission prompt or question is waiting for input. Requires Claude Code v2.1.119 or later. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings#available-settings

settings.title: Managed Mcp Servers
settings.description: (Managed settings, third-party Desktop deployments only) MCP server configurations pushed to all users. Each entry specifies the transport and connection details, plus an optional toolPolicy map. Delivered through the managed settings file or MDM. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/desktop#enterprise-configuration

settings.title: Path
settings.description: Absolute path to the policy helper executable
settings.writeReason: Executable callbacks require a dedicated reviewed workflow.

settings.title: Permission Denied
settings.description: Hooks that run when a tool call is denied by the auto mode classifier. Supports matchers on tool name. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks
settings.writeReason: This structured value contains provider-owned authentication fields.

settings.title: Permission Request
settings.description: Hooks that run when a permission dialog appears
settings.writeReason: This structured value contains provider-owned authentication fields.

settings.title: Plugin Suggestion Marketplaces
settings.description: (Managed settings only) Allowlist of marketplace names whose plugins may surface as contextual "suggested for this directory" tips in the /plugin Discover tab. No marketplace-declared suggestions surface without this allowlist. The built-in first-party frontend-design tip is unaffected. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings#available-settings

settings.title: Plugin Trust Message
settings.description: (Managed settings only) Custom message appended to the plugin trust warning shown before installation. Use to provide organization-specific context about approved plugins. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings#plugin-settings

settings.title: Preferred Notif Channel
settings.description: Method for task-complete and permission-prompt notifications. "auto" (default) sends a desktop notification in iTerm2, Ghostty, and Kitty and does nothing in other terminals; "terminal_bell" rings the bell in any terminal; "notifications_disabled" turns them off. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/terminal-config#get-a-terminal-bell-or-notification
settings.writeReason: Workspace owns this launch overlay so pane attention remains accurate.

settings.title: Refresh Interval Ms
settings.description: How often in milliseconds to re-run the helper in the background; set to 0 to disable refresh, or to at least 60000
settings.writeReason: Executable callbacks require a dedicated reviewed workflow.

settings.title: Show Clear Context On Plan Accept
settings.description: When true, the plan-approval dialog offers a "clear context" option. Defaults to false.

settings.title: Skip Dangerous Mode Permission Prompt
settings.description: Whether the user has accepted the bypass permissions mode dialog. Typically managed by the CLI rather than set by hand.

settings.title: Ssh Host Allowlist
settings.description: (Managed settings only) Allowlist restricting Desktop SSH sessions to approved hosts. Patterns are case-insensitive; * matches any host and *.example.com matches example.com and any subdomain. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/desktop#ssh-sessions

settings.title: Strict Known Marketplaces
settings.description: (Managed settings only) Allowlist of plugin marketplaces users can add. Undefined = no restrictions, empty array = lockdown. Uses exact matching for source specifications. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugin-marketplaces#managed-marketplace-restrictions
settings.writeReason: This structured value contains provider-owned authentication fields.

settings.title: Timeout Ms
settings.description: How long in milliseconds to wait for the helper before treating the run as failed
settings.writeReason: Executable callbacks require a dedicated reviewed workflow.

settings.title: Use Auto Mode During Plan
settings.description: When true, apply the auto mode classifier during plan mode to auto-approve safe read-only tool calls while planning. Has no effect unless permissions.defaultMode allows auto. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/permissions

settings.title: Wsl Inherits Windows Settings
settings.description: (Windows managed settings only) When true, Claude Code on WSL reads managed settings from the Windows policy chain in addition to /etc/claude-code, with Windows sources taking priority. Only honored when set in the HKLM registry key or C:\Program Files\ClaudeCode\managed-settings.json, both of which require Windows admin to write. For HKCU policy to also apply on WSL, the flag must additionally be set in HKCU itself. Has no effect on native Windows. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings#available-settings

settings.title: ANTHROPIC VERTEX PROJECT ID
settings.description: Google Vertex AI project ID

settings.title: Auto Updates Channel
settings.description: Release channel to follow for updates. Use "stable" for a version that is typically about one week old and skips versions with major regressions, or "latest" (default) for the most recent release. Set DISABLE_AUTOUPDATER=1 to disable background auto-updates, or DISABLE_UPDATES=1 (added in v2.1.118) to block all update paths including manual `claude update`.

settings.title: Blocked Marketplaces
settings.description: (Managed settings only) Blocklist of marketplace sources. These exact sources are blocked from being added as marketplaces. The check happens before downloading, so blocked sources never touch the filesystem.

settings.title: CLAUDE CODE DISABLE GIT INSTRUCTIONS
